title: astragalus psammophilus
intro: Astragalus psammophilus, also known as the sand-pea, is a type of perennial legume native to the deserts and dry regions of the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The health benefits of Astragalus psammophilus are said to include reducing stress and fatigue, improving immune function, and supporting cardiovascular health. Astragalus psammophilus can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its effects.
